member.name: F:B2R2.MiddleEnd.DataFlow.TopologicalDataFlowAnalysis`2.ins
member.summary: Entry lattice per vertex.

member.name: F:B2R2.MiddleEnd.DataFlow.TopologicalDataFlowAnalysis`2.outs
member.summary: Exit lattice per vertex.

member.name: M:B2R2.MiddleEnd.DataFlow.TopologicalDataFlowAnalysis`2.Transfer(`0,B2R2.MiddleEnd.BinGraph.Vertex{`1})
member.summary: The transfer function from an input lattice to an output lattice. The second parameter is to specify the current block of interest.

member.name: M:B2R2.MiddleEnd.DataFlow.TopologicalDataFlowAnalysis`2.Meet(`0,`0)
member.summary: Meet operation of the lattice.

member.name: M:B2R2.MiddleEnd.DataFlow.TopologicalDataFlowAnalysis`2.InitInsOuts``1(B2R2.MiddleEnd.BinGraph.DiGraph{`1,``0},B2R2.MiddleEnd.BinGraph.Vertex{`1})
member.summary: Initialize ints and outs.

member.name: M:B2R2.MiddleEnd.DataFlow.TopologicalDataFlowAnalysis`2.Compute``1(B2R2.MiddleEnd.BinGraph.DiGraph{`1,``0},B2R2.MiddleEnd.BinGraph.Vertex{`1})
member.summary: Compute data-flow with the iterative worklist algorithm.

member.name: T:B2R2.MiddleEnd.DataFlow.TopologicalDataFlowAnalysis`2
member.summary: Classic data-flow analysis with topological worklist algorithm.

member.name: P:B2R2.MiddleEnd.DataFlow.DataFlowAnalysis`2.Top
member.summary: The top of the lattice. A data-flow analysis solution is computed by iterating down from top to bottom.

member.name: T:B2R2.MiddleEnd.DataFlow.DataFlowAnalysis`2
member.summary: Data-flow analysis framework. 'L is a lattice, 'V is a vertex data type of a graph.

member.name: T:B2R2.MiddleEnd.DataFlow.DataFlowDirection
member.summary: Either forward or backward analysis.

member.name: T:B2R2.MiddleEnd.DataFlow.VarPoint
member.summary: Program point of a defined variable.

member.name: T:B2R2.MiddleEnd.DataFlow.VarExpr
member.summary: Defined variable.

member.name: P:B2R2.MiddleEnd.DataFlow.CPState`1.SSAWorkList
member.summary: Worklist for SSA stmt, this stack stores a list of def variables, and we will use SSAEdges to find all related SSA statements.

member.name: P:B2R2.MiddleEnd.DataFlow.CPState`1.FlowWorkList
member.summary: Worklist for blocks.

member.name: P:B2R2.MiddleEnd.DataFlow.CPState`1.DefaultWordSize
member.summary: Default word size of the current analysis.

member.name: P:B2R2.MiddleEnd.DataFlow.CPState`1.ExecutedEdges
member.summary: Executed edges from vid to vid.

member.name: P:B2R2.MiddleEnd.DataFlow.CPState`1.ExecutableEdges
member.summary: Executable edges from vid to vid. If there's no element for an edge, that means the edge is not executable.

member.name: P:B2R2.MiddleEnd.DataFlow.CPState`1.MemState
member.summary: SSA mem values. Only store values of constant addresses.

member.name: P:B2R2.MiddleEnd.DataFlow.CPState`1.RegState
member.summary: SSA var values.

member.name: P:B2R2.MiddleEnd.DataFlow.CPState`1.SSAEdges
member.summary: SSA edges

member.name: P:B2R2.MiddleEnd.DataFlow.CPState`1.BinHandle
member.summary: BinHandle of the current binary.

member.name: T:B2R2.MiddleEnd.DataFlow.SSAMemID
member.summary: An ID of an SSA memory instance.

member.name: T:B2R2.MiddleEnd.DataFlow.ConstantPropagation`1
member.summary: Modified version of sparse conditional constant propagation of Wegman et al.

member.name: T:B2R2.MiddleEnd.DataFlow.StackPointerPropagation
member.summary: Variant of Constant Propagation. It only cares stack-related registers: stack pointer and frame pointer.

member.name: T:B2R2.MiddleEnd.DataFlow.ConstantCopyPropagation
member.summary: Variant of Constant Propagation. It only cares the copy of constants. When the GOT pointer is calculated for x86-pie binaries, it only considers the expression (adding thunk pointer and offset) other than Num.

member.name: T:B2R2.MiddleEnd.DataFlow.UntouchedValuePropagation
member.summary: Modified version of sparse conditional constant propagation of Wegman et al.

member.name: M:B2R2.MiddleEnd.DataFlow.DataFlowChainModule.filterLastDefInBlock(Microsoft.FSharp.Collections.FSharpSet{B2R2.MiddleEnd.DataFlow.VarPoint})
member.summary: When there are more than one defs for the same variable, we should choose the last one.
Merge memory with pre-calculated merge points
Merge memory mapping and return true if changed.

member.name: M:B2R2.MiddleEnd.DataFlow.SCCPTransfer.isGetPCThunk(B2R2.FrontEnd.BinInterface.BinHandle,System.UInt64)
member.summary: This is a heuristic to discover __x86.get_pc_thunk- family functions. 1. If a function name symbol exists and its name matches, then we know it is __x86.get_pc_thunk- family 2. But there are some cases we don't have symbols for them. In such cases, we directly compare first 4 bytes of byte code. Because __x86.get_pc_thunk- family only has 4 bytes for its function body and their values are fixed.

member.name: M:B2R2.MiddleEnd.DataFlow.StackState.computeMemoryMergePoints(B2R2.MiddleEnd.BinEssence.BinEssence,B2R2.MiddleEnd.BinGraph.DiGraph{B2R2.MiddleEnd.Lens.SSABBlock,B2R2.MiddleEnd.BinEssence.CFGEdgeKind},B2R2.MiddleEnd.DataFlow.CPState{B2R2.MiddleEnd.DataFlow.StackValue})
member.summary: Memory merge point means an address of which the merging operation should be performed. Mempory merge point is necessary for dataflow analysis on SSA. Without this, every time we meet a phi statement of a memory, we should iterate memories and merge values at all exisitng addresses.

member.name: M:B2R2.MiddleEnd.DataFlow.CopyTransfer.isGetPCThunk(B2R2.FrontEnd.BinInterface.BinHandle,System.UInt64)
member.summary: This is a heuristic to discover __x86.get_pc_thunk- family functions. 1. If a function name symbol exists and its name matches, then we know it is __x86.get_pc_thunk- family 2. But there are some cases we don't have symbols for them. In such cases, we directly compare first 4 bytes of byte code. Because __x86.get_pc_thunk- family only has 4 bytes for its function body and their values are fixed.
